Disposition_Rationale: Keep; DOAB metadata feeds are explicitly CC0.
Merged_Notes: Global aggregator of peer-reviewed open access books; highly structured metadata useful for discovering academic and open-science book awards.

Disposition_Rationale: Keep; DPLA metadata policy dedicates metadata to CC0/public domain.
Merged_Notes: A US national digital library that aggregates metadata from many institutions, with linked data potential. || Aggregates millions of records from US libraries; excellent REST API for tracking regional American literary and historical society awards.

Alias_Names: Europeana API | Europeana Linked Open Data
Disposition_Rationale: Keep; Europeana metadata is CC0, but credit to providers/aggregators is strongly requested.
Merged_Notes: A vast cultural heritage aggregator. Its dataset includes millions of digital objects, many of which are books and may contain award-related metadata. || Aggregates metadata from thousands of European cultural institutions. All metadata is CC0, though linked digital objects vary.

Alias_Names: HathiTrust Bibliographic API | HathiTrust Digital Library
Disposition_Rationale: Keep; HathiTrust metadata is shared under CC0, but attribution is requested as community norm.
Merged_Notes: A large-scale collaborative repository of digital content from research libraries, with linked data capabilities. || Access to brief bibliographic records for millions of digitized volumes; useful for cross-referencing historic prize discovery.

Disposition_Rationale: Keep; official IdRef says open reuse under Etalab and exposes triple store/OAI-PMH/APIs.
Merged_Notes: A major authority hub for French higher education and research. Its data is used in projects linking authors and their awards. || Francophone higher education authority file; an excellent complement to BnF for academic and scholarly awards.

Alias_Names: LIBRIS (National Library of Sweden) | LIBRIS Sweden
Disposition_Rationale: Keep; official Libris docs say national bibliography and authority records are CC0.
Merged_Notes: Pioneering library LOD; definitive source for Nordic prizes and comprehensive Nobel metadata. || The Swedish union catalog, which is transitioning to BIBFRAME-based linked data. It is a key source for Swedish publications and their associated awards.

Canonical_Source: National Library of New Zealand API
Alias_Names: National Library of New Zealand API
Disposition_Rationale: Keep as dataset-family entry, not a blanket source row; Publications NZ metadata is CC BY with attribution.
Merged_Notes: Rich source for discovering Oceania, Māori, and Pacific literary awards; exposes metadata in structured formats.

Disposition_Rationale: Keep; rewrite licence to CC0 per Open Library FAQ, not generic 'Public Domain'.
Merged_Notes: Contains award data parsed from MARC records; strong for Anglosphere trade books and market discovery. || While not purely a linked data endpoint, Open Library's data dumps and APIs contain extensive award information for books. It is a major source for connecting works to awards like the Booker Prize.

Disposition_Rationale: Keep as secondary scholarly graph; CC-BY reuse with acknowledgement, not a direct prize registry.
Merged_Notes: Massive open access European scholarly graph; highly effective for scraping structural data on academic monograph prizes and subsidized publication awards.

Disposition_Rationale: Keep; VIAF under ODC-By with attribution guidance; use mainly as identity bridge.
Merged_Notes: An international authority file that aggregates authority data from many national libraries, crucial for linking authors and works to awards. || Essential for cross-walking national authority records; clusters representations of literary prizes and laureates globally.

Disposition_Rationale: Keep; lobid-gnd exposes CC0 GND data via JSON-LD API.
Merged_Notes: Provides a highly accessible, developer-friendly JSON-LD API over the German Authority File (GND) for querying German-speaking world prizes.

Disposition_Rationale: Keep; Crossref metadata is open and mostly unrestricted, but note nuance around abstracts and Crossref-generated data.
Best_Current_License: Open / mostly unrestricted; CC0 for Crossref-generated and OFR
Merged_Notes: Massive REST API for scholarly books and monographs globally; funding and award nodes frequently surface academic book prizes.

Disposition_Rationale: Keep only as discovery layer, not core trust-tier source.
Observed_License_Claims: CC BY-SA | CC BY-SA 3.0 / GNU FDL
Merged_Notes: A massive, community-driven knowledge graph extracted from Wikipedia. Contains structured information on a vast number of awards. || Ideal for extracting structured data from Wikipedia category trees for prizes not yet fully modeled in Wikidata.

Canonical_Source: Enciclopedia de la Literatura en México (ELEM)
Alias_Names: Enciclopedia de la Literatura en México (ELEM)
Disposition_Rationale: Useful reference pool, but mixed licensing means not core-safe without a tighter rights model.
Merged_Notes: Essential mapping source for Mexican book awards and literary academies; underlying facts and identifiers are highly valuable despite mixed text licensing.

Disposition_Rationale: Do not keep blanket CC0/No attribution claim; treat as conditional until specific ISNI data lane is confirmed.
Merged_Notes: An ISO standard for identifying public identities of contributors to creative works, useful for linking authors to awards. || Global standard for identifying public identities and organizations; critical for disambiguating award-granting bodies.

Alias_Names: LC Linked Data Service: Awards & Prizes | Library of Congress LDS
Disposition_Rationale: Do not keep 'Public Domain/No attribution' as final without explicit licence capture from an official rights page.
Merged_Notes: Core Anglosphere name and subject authorities; standardizes English-language award names. || The Library of Congress offers a dedicated Linked Data vocabulary for awards and prizes. It includes specific authorities for winners of major literary awards like the PEN America Literary Award and Women's Prize for Fiction.

Disposition_Rationale: Seed row indicates non-commercial default with commercial application required.
Merged_Notes: Best for East Asian discovery; needs application for bulk commercial use.

Canonical_Source: National Library of Scotland Data Foundry
Alias_Names: National Library of Scotland Data Foundry
Disposition_Rationale: Keep as dataset-family source only; NLS Data Foundry rights vary by dataset, not blanket CC0.
Merged_Notes: Provides bulk metadata downloads in MARC/XML; vital for capturing Scottish literary history and regional Anglosphere awards.

Disposition_Rationale: Keep as dataset family; individual Post45 datasets should be tracked dataset-by-dataset.
Observed_License_Claims: CC0 / CC BY | Varies (likely CC BY)
Merged_Notes: Curates datasets on post-1945 literature and culture, including specific sets on US and UK literary prizes and their winners. || Highly structured datasets for post-1945 Anglosphere literature; provides excellent reconciliation targets for contemporary literary awards and market data.

Disposition_Rationale: Usable as historical indirect discovery support, but not a primary prize source.
Merged_Notes: A volunteer effort to digitize and archive cultural works, with potential linked data applications. || Provides bulk RDF/XML dumps; useful for historical baseline mapping of classic Anglosphere literature.

Disposition_Rationale: Keep as secondary/conditional; Trove API covers metadata only, requires API key, attribution, and has usage conditions.
Merged_Notes: An Australian online library database aggregator, with linked data capabilities. || Essential node for Australian and Oceania literary awards; API requires registration for bulk data.

Alias_Names: BNE Spain | National Library of Spain (BNE)
Disposition_Rationale: Carried forward from the seed list without additional re-audit in this consolidation.
Merged_Notes: Provides a rich LOD dataset with millions of authority and bibliographic records, including award information. || Spanish national bibliography and Ibero-American awards.
